hypothesis: Defer every is_reduce_only order by 50 ms via a clock alert so residual oracle drift accrues to the closed position's realized P&L. Opening orders pass through unchanged.
notes: Train P&L delta is -0.77% vs the +5% gate (FAIL, not CLOSE; gap=5.77pp > close_margin_pct=2.0). Slippage uninformative — fill model reports 0.0 for both algos on every date (see research/NOTES.md). Win-rate rose +6-9pp under the defer but mean dollar P&L did not, suggesting the defer redistributes outcomes without enlarging them. Trade count drops ~25% because some deferred closes get superseded by later strategy reversals — the algo isn't a pure scheduling overlay. Test split is only 1 date (cached only — see RESULT WARNING in research/NOTES.md); not a basis for OOS claims either way.
